event: hypothyroidism following total thyroidectomy for a multinodular goitre
no history of obstructive sleep apnoea
hypoparathyroidism well managed on long term oral calcium carbonate and calcitriol
converted to emergency Roux-en-Y gastric bypass surgery
patient weighed 95 kg with a BMI of 38.5
transfer to intensive care at a tertiary hospital
continuous intravenous calcium gluconate infusion required
received all medication and nutrition intravenously
Endocrinology advice required throughout her admission
two weeks into her admission, Endocrinology advice sought regarding hypothyroidism
euthyroidism achieved after gradual up titration to 40/20/40 µg daily
six weeks into her admission, intravenous calcitriol 1 µg alternate daily commenced
eight weeks into her admission, 150000 units of intramuscular cholecalciferol trialled
low 1,25(OH)vitamin D3 level failed to increment
three months into her admission, intravenous calcitriol recommenced at 1 µg alternate daily
reduced calcium gluconate requirements to 4.4 mmol three times per day
IV thyroxine 200 µg alternate daily commenced 4 days later
maintenance schedule established controlling hypocalcaemia and hypothyroidism
prolonged hospital stay of over 8 months
increased dose of calcium 1200 mg orally twice daily
remained well replaced with normocalcaemia on review 6 weeks after discharge
remained well replaced with normocalcaemia on review 12 weeks after discharge
remained well replaced with normocalcaemia on review 4 months after discharge
weighed 71.8 kg and had a BMI of 29
